message:
fix(sync/execute_flush): hoist conflict-marker check above the main flush path too

Follow-up to the previous execute_flush tombstone-preservation commit
that added the conflict-marker pre-scan, but only on the "nothing to
flush" early-return branch. The main flush path immediately below
rewrites the JSONL via `export_result`, and if the existing JSONL
contained unresolved `<<<<<<<` / `=======` / `>>>>>>>` markers, that
rewrite would silently resolve the conflict in favor of whatever is
currently in the local DB (effectively dropping the remote side of
the merge).

Move the `ensure_no_conflict_markers` call out of the early-return
branch and up to the top of `execute_flush`, gated on
`jsonl_exists && !args.force`. That way every flush path — the
nothing-to-do short-circuit, the incremental flush, and the
full-export flush — trips on the markers before we touch the file.
Keep the comment on the early-return branch in place so a reader
still knows why the divergence/empty-DB safety checks below can
trust the JSONL to be parseable, but update it to reflect that the
check now runs upstream.

`--force` continues to be the escape hatch for operators who
genuinely want the local DB to overwrite a merge-conflicted JSONL
(e.g. during a deliberate rebuild from a clean DB snapshot).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
